reasoning_model_1: The audio correctly synthesizes the text content '比赛进行到关键时刻，他带球突破，射门进了，太精彩了'. However, regarding the core paralinguistic requirement 'start with medium pitch, then gradually increase to extremely high pitch', the model performs poorly. The audio maintains a relatively flat, standard audiobook-style narration tone throughout (00:00-00:05). Instead of raising the pitch for the exciting climax ('射门！进了！'), the voice actually employs a falling intonation at the end of the sentence ('太精彩了'), which lowers the pitch. There is no perceptible gradual increase in pitch, nor is there any 'extremely high pitch' used. The emotion is too calm for a sports commentary context.
reasoning_model_2: The model accurately generates the text content. In terms of the core requirement, it demonstrates a clear dynamic adjustment. The audio begins with an energetic but controlled medium-high pitch (00:00-00:02). As the action progresses ('他带球突破...'), the tension builds. Crucially, at the climax (00:03-00:04) on the words '进了！', the pitch and volume jump significantly, simulating a shouting commentator style, effectively reaching a high pitch. The final phrase '太精彩了' maintains high energy. The model successfully conveys the 'gradual increase' followed by 'extremely high pitch' (relative to normal speech) required for the sports commentary context.
model_comparison: Model 1 failed to adhere to the paralinguistic instruction regarding pitch. It produced a standard, flat reading with falling intonation at the end, completely missing the requested gradual rise to an extremely high pitch. Model 2, in contrast, effectively adopted the persona of a sports commentator. It managed the pitch progression well, starting with anticipation and culminating in a high-pitched shout at the climax ('进了！'), satisfying the prompt's requirement for dynamic pitch adjustment. Model 2 is the clear winner.
